# Evidence Kernels

| Kernel | Name | Claim | Supports | Source IDs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| K01 | Compression Load Reduction | If the viewer needs fewer words to extract the same idea, comprehension and effort can improve. | SCC, CRL, AOO, MFC | S02; S04; S05 |
| K02 | Structured Signaling | Cues that reveal organization reduce search and support retention/transfer. | OFC, PCFB, CPC, PMM | S06 |
| K03 | Sparse Attention Anchoring | Highlighting can guide attention only if sparse and meaningful. | BCC, SKH, BCA, FOA | S06; S14; S18 |
| K04 | Dual-Layer Access Preservation | Creative overlays must not replace full captions for access. | DLC, CCP, ACAC | S01; S02; S17 |
| K05 | Muted-First Resilience | Sound-off viewing shifts load to subtitles, so overlays must be self-contained. | MFC, BCC+, DLC | S03 |
| K06 | Gaze-Safe Placement | Text should not block regions viewers need for comprehension: face, mouth, hands, product, action, UI. | GSP, MSP, ASP, USP | S07; S12; S13 |
| K07 | Crowding Avoidance | Recognition worsens when text is near clutter, UI, or competing elements. | CAC, CBU, SOF, ACAC | S08; S17 |
| K08 | Foveal-Peripheral Architecture | Detailed text belongs near foveal fixation; periphery should use simple cues. | FPA, PIL, IWP | S08; S09 |
| K09 | Visual Working Memory Chunking | Short semantic beats fit memory limits better than long sentence blocks. | OIOS, PCFB, SBT | S10 |
| K10 | Predictive Timing | Semantic pre-cues may reduce prediction load if not mistimed. | PSP, SLSB, PCC | S03; S06; S22 |
| K11 | Kinetic Restraint | Motion helps when it encodes structure/tone; constant motion creates load. | LKM, DMC, KT | S16 |
| K12 | Affective-Semantic Encoding | Typography can encode tone/emotion where tone is part of meaning. | ASE, ESC, ASC | S19 |
| K13 | Platform-Native Fit | Vertical video UI and scroll behavior require platform-specific safe zones and testing. | USP, PMM, ERF | S20; S24 |
| K14 | Adaptive Routing | No method is globally optimal; routing by context can outperform a global default. | ASOE, CER, MFM | S20; S23 |
| K15 | Proof Integrity | Method claims must be falsifiable, preregistered, and tied to practical significance. | PFT, EXP suite | S20; S21; S25 |
